## For health professionals: Hepatitis E
Get detailed information on hepatitis E for health professionals.
- What health professionals need to know about hepatitis E
- For more information

## What health professionals need to know about hepatitis E
Hepatitis E virus is classified as a virus belonging to the genus Orthohepevirus in the family Hepeviridae.
Monitor for symptoms of disease. Confirm using serological or nucleic acid tests, and by exclusion of hepatitis A, B and C viruses.
Serological tests involve enzyme-linked immunosorbent assays (ELISA) for the detection of antibodies to hepatitis E virus (IgM, IgA, and IgG).
Nucleic acid tests involve reverse transcription-polymerase chain reaction (RT-PCR) assays for the detection of hepatitis E virus RNA in samples of:
No specific treatment is currently available. Those infected should recover on their own with bed rest.
